title: My photography project
content_html:
My name’s Martin Cabello and I’m a young photographer. Taking photographs was always my favourite activity as a child. And when I was 15, I felt that if I wanted to take my hobby to the next level, I had to do something special. So I decided to start a personal project: I would take one photograph every day for a whole year of my life. I knew that many other young photographers before me had done the same thing, but it seemed like a really great idea, and so I started.

After a few weeks, I decided to put these photos onto a website for young photographers because I was keen to create a photo diary, and also to have the chance to show other people my photos. I was amazed to discover that there were others who liked the way I saw and photographed things. I was also enjoying photography a wide range of subjects. Then someone suggested that if my photographs all had a common topic or idea to connect them, my project would be even better – and I agreed.

So I began to concentrate on taking photos of nature scenes, especially of birds and the sky. A lot of them were black and white photographs, which was very different from what many other young photographers were doing – they were all taking colour photos. As a result, I sometimes had doubts about my project, and I almost gave up several times. In the end, however, I trusted my own feelings, and became more confident about my photography style and choices.

One day I showed my project to my art teacher at school. She told me about a national photography competition for people my age. So I entered some of my photos, just for fun. I’ll never forget the moment when the judges told me that I had won first prize; I was so happy, as my project meant so much to me. It was the first time I realised that maybe my photography really was good, and that I might actually be able to work as a professional photographer. I now have my own photography studio and I’ve never regretted my career choice.


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: Martin started his project because he wanted to

options.A: influence other photographers.
options.B: try an original idea of his own.
options.C: take a lot more photos than he had before.
options.D: develop his photography further.

questions.explanation: 11. D: Trong đoạn 1, Martin nói rằng 'if I wanted to take my hobby to the next level, I had to do something special' (nếu muốn đưa sở thích của mình lên một tầm cao mới, tôi phải làm điều gì đó đặc biệt), đồng nghĩa với việc phát triển kỹ năng nhiếp ảnh xa hơn.

questions.text: What happened after Martin put his photos on the website?

options.A: He became more interested in other photographers’ pictures.
options.B: He was given some advice to improve his project.
options.C: He decided that his photos weren’t good enough.
options.D: He discovered the website wasn’t the best place for his photos.

questions.explanation: 12. B: Đoạn 2 đề cập 'Then someone suggested that if my photographs all had a common topic... my project would be even better' (Sau đó có người gợi ý rằng nếu các bức ảnh của tôi có chung một chủ đề... dự án của tôi sẽ còn tuyệt hơn).

questions.text: How did Martin feel when he took his first nature photos?

options.A: He wasn’t sure that he was doing the right thing.
options.B: He was certain that the photos would be better in colour.
options.C: He was anxious about what others would think.
options.D: He was worried that his photos might not be interesting.

questions.explanation: 13. A: Trong đoạn 3, vì phong cách chụp đen trắng khác biệt so với mọi người, Martin 'sometimes had doubts about my project, and I almost gave up' (đôi khi có những hoài nghi về dự án và suýt bỏ cuộc vài lần).

questions.text: How did winning the competition affect Martin?

options.A: He realised how enjoyable competitions can be.
options.B: It helped him to make a decision about his future.
options.C: It gave him the opportunity to meet more young photographers.
options.D: He started putting more effort into his photography.

questions.explanation: 14. B: Đoạn cuối viết 'It was the first time I realised... that I might actually be able to work as a professional photographer' (Đó là lần đầu tiên tôi nhận ra mình có thể làm việc như một nhiếp ảnh gia chuyên nghiệp), giúp anh đưa ra quyết định nghề nghiệp.

questions.text: What would be a good title for this text?

options.A: My favourite websites for sharing photographs.
options.B: The best way to become a successful young photographer.
options.C: How a photography project changed my life.
options.D: Why more photographers should take pictures of nature.

questions.explanation: 15. C: Toàn bộ bài đọc kể về hành trình từ lúc Martin bắt đầu một dự án ảnh cá nhân cho đến khi dự án đó giúp anh định hướng trở thành một nhiếp ảnh gia chuyên nghiệp.

title: Rock the Planet
content_html:
Rock the Planet, a musical show where actors also have to be really good musicians, opened in London last year, after five years in the USA, where it was voted ‘Best Musical’ four times! But doing the show with an all-British cast of actors was challenging. The UK puts more limits on how many performances young actors can do weekly – in the USA, they’re allowed to do more shows. Because of this, twice as many actors were required here. Luckily, the director found plenty of talented UK-based actors, and they’ve just won their fifth ‘Best Musical’ award! Although the theatre in London is bigger than the US venue, it’s been full every night, with everyone wanting to hear the songs that first became popular in America.

I met 11-year-old Jamie, one of the show’s actors. ‘It’s brilliant that I get paid to play,’ he said, ‘especially a beautiful and expensive Gibson guitar.’ He told me how busy the young actors are. ‘We have normal school lessons each day at the theatre. While the teachers are great, I miss my old school mates. And what do they think of the show? Well, my whole class came! The theatre does special deals on tickets for schools. They absolutely loved it and that made me feel really confident. My parents come when they can, but it’s expensive for them,’ he explained.

When I said I also play guitar, Jamie found a spare one and invited me to play along with him and the other young actors. We played Days, a song I wasn’t familiar with. Jamie took me through it and I was very pleased with myself when I finally got it right. I was impressed by Jamie’s ability and wished I had similar skills. I was just trying to keep up and was glad the show’s director wasn’t there to hear my mistakes!

While the young actors were already musicians, with some playing several instruments, none had played in a band before. Fortunately, they’ve found it pretty easy. Jamie admitted that the hard part was appearing natural when they first performed together. They were concentrating so much that they forgot to smile!

The show’s on in London for another year, and is well worth seeing.


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: What is the difference between the shows in the UK and in the USA?

options.A: Fewer young actors were needed in America.
options.B: Brand new songs have been included in the UK.
options.C: The UK show has won more prizes.
options.D: Audiences were bigger in America.

questions.explanation: 11. A: Đoạn 1 nêu rõ 'twice as many actors were required here' (tại Anh cần số lượng diễn viên gấp đôi) do giới hạn số buổi diễn của diễn viên nhí, đồng nghĩa với việc ở Mỹ cần ít diễn viên nhí hơn.

questions.text: What does Jamie say about working on the show?

options.A: He’s glad that his family come daily to support him.
options.B: He’s embarrassed when his friends come to watch him.
options.C: He’s delighted that he’s doing something that he loves.
options.D: He’s anxious about the amount of schoolwork he’s missing.

questions.explanation: 12. C: Trong đoạn 2, Jamie hào hứng chia sẻ: 'It’s brilliant that I get paid to play... especially a beautiful and expensive Gibson guitar' (Thật tuyệt vời khi tôi được trả tiền để chơi đàn).

questions.text: How did the writer feel about playing music with the actors?

options.A: surprised that he sounded as good as Jamie
options.B: satisfied that he had learned a new song
options.C: happy because they played his favourite tune
options.D: excited to be playing in front of the director

questions.explanation: 13. B: Ở đoạn 3, tác giả nói rằng bài hát 'Days' là bài anh chưa biết, và anh 'very pleased with myself when I finally got it right' (rất hài lòng với bản thân khi cuối cùng cũng chơi đúng).

questions.text: What did the actors find difficult about performing in the show?

options.A: having to play their instruments with other people
options.B: learning to play many new instruments very quickly
options.C: remembering the different songs they had to do
options.D: looking relaxed when they were playing on stage

questions.explanation: 14. D: Đoạn 4 ghi rõ: 'Jamie admitted that the hard part was appearing natural... They were concentrating so much that they forgot to smile!' (Jamie thừa nhận phần khó nhất là tỏ ra tự nhiên... họ quên cả mỉm cười).

questions.text: What would an advertisement for the show say?

options.A: Come and enjoy this wonderful show while you can – tickets are selling fast…
options.B: Book to see Rock the Planet for an experience to remember…
options.C: You won’t often see something as good as Rock the Planet…
options.D: After its success in several countries…

evidenceKeywords: actors also have to be really good musicians
questions.explanation: 15. B: Xuyên suốt bài viết ca ngợi chất lượng âm nhạc và kỹ năng biểu diễn trực tiếp của các diễn viên nhí, đoạn kết khẳng định show diễn 'is well worth seeing' (rất đáng xem), phù hợp với thông điệp quảng cáo một trải nghiệm đáng nhớ.

title: The teenage snowboarder
content_html:
15-year-old Vanessa Hanson writes about her sport.

I was born in Denver, USA, and started snowboarding aged eight when my dad took me to a resort in the mountains, about two hours from Denver. The hill looked unbelievably steep and scary, so my dad had to do a lot of persuading to get me on a board. Because I was so young, I hadn’t even thought of asking him if I could start snowboarding, but he was already into it and wanted me to love it too – he made it look so simple. And in the end, I managed just fine.

After this experience, it wasn’t long before I developed a love of the sport and had improved my ability too. I started competing aged ten. Some people enter loads of competitions and end up getting bored of snowboarding, but my love of taking part, and winning too, if I’m honest, has got stronger as I’ve gotten older, which always helps me to push myself harder. I enjoy not having a fixed plan, and sometimes this goes well, like when I became the first female ever to get a perfect 100 at a local competition. I wasn’t sure I’d even attempt the jumps that got me this score until I actually set off. That achievement increased my trust in my own abilities.

During the summer, I focus on training and my studies while there’s no snow. Although I train daily, it’s nice not having to stick to quite a strict diet like I do in the winter. I also get to spend lots of time with my friends then. It’s the best way of dealing with not being able to do what I love so much and what I’m really good at.

I have to think carefully about my future. Snowboarding is such a huge part of my life now, but I also feel it’s important to have something else I can do, just in case I don’t succeed as a professional snowboarder or if I get injured. I’m wondering whether becoming a doctor is a possibility, if my active life allows time for doing a college degree. It’s impossible to imagine myself growing tired of the sport, but I can’t go on snowboarding my whole life.


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: Just before Vanessa snowboarded down a hill for the first time, she felt

options.A: worried she was too young.
options.B: nervous about doing it.
options.C: keen to copy her father.
options.D: pleased to be allowed to do it.

questions.explanation: 11. B: Trong đoạn 1, Vanessa miêu tả ngọn đồi trông 'unbelievably steep and scary' (dốc và đáng sợ đến khó tin), khiến bố cô phải thuyết phục rất nhiều, chứng tỏ cô bé cảm thấy lo lắng/sợ hãi.

questions.text: What does Vanessa say about taking part in competitions?

options.A: She expects too much of herself.
options.B: She sometimes loses interest.
options.C: She always plans for them carefully.
options.D: She enjoys them more and more.

questions.explanation: 12. D: Đoạn 2 ghi rõ: 'my love of taking part... has got stronger as I’ve gotten older' (tình yêu đối với việc tham gia thi đấu... ngày càng lớn mạnh hơn khi tôi lớn lên).

questions.text: In the summer, Vanessa enjoys

options.A: the good weather where she lives.
options.B: not having to train regularly.
options.C: being able to eat whatever she likes.
options.D: having plenty of time to do her schoolwork.

questions.explanation: 13. C: Trong đoạn 3, Vanessa đề cập vào mùa hè 'it’s nice not having to stick to quite a strict diet like I do in the winter' (thật tuyệt khi không phải tuân thủ chế độ ăn kiêng nghiêm ngặt như mùa đông).

questions.text: Vanessa says that in the future, snowboarding

options.A: may not be her main career.
options.B: will be a way for her to make a lot of money.
options.C: might not always be interesting for her.
options.D: will help her stay fit and healthy.

evidenceKeywords: becoming a doctor is a possibility
questions.explanation: 14. A: Đoạn cuối, Vanessa cho rằng điều quan trọng là phải có một nghề khác 'just in case I don't succeed as a professional snowboarder' và cô đang cân nhắc việc trở thành bác sĩ.

questions.text: What would Vanessa say to one of her friends?

options.A: I love snowboarding, but it’s a shame I have so many accidents.
options.B: I’m pleased that I’m more confident now…
options.C: I’m so happy to be a champion snowboarder…
options.D: I’m sorry I’ve never been able to see you that much…

questions.explanation: 15. B: Ở đoạn 2, cô chia sẻ việc đạt được điểm tuyệt đối 100 đã 'increased my trust in my own abilities' (tăng sự tin tưởng vào khả năng của chính mình), đồng nghĩa với việc tự tin hơn.

title: Challenge yourself!
content_html:
15-year-old Kyle Washington writes about what he does in his free time.

I joined the National Youth Challenge (NYC) last year because I knew they went camping and sailing and taught skills like public speaking, and these were things I’d never done before. In the NYC you can do different activities to challenge yourself and you’re always learning something. There are different levels, from beginner to advanced, run by adult volunteers called ‘leaders’. When you complete each level you get a certificate.

When I first joined, my leader suggested that my first challenge should be helping to organise activities at a local children’s club for two hours a week. I wasn’t sure it was a good idea; I’d joined that same club when I was eight and I’d found it really difficult to make friends. I was quite shy then and all the other children already had their own friendship groups, so I’d stopped going after a couple of weeks. I decided to take up the challenge though, but only because I wanted my first NYC certificate.

Soon, however, I wanted to spend all my free time at the children’s club. It meant a lot more to me than just getting the certificate. And when I finished the challenge, the NYC leader let me keep on volunteering at the club because I’d built up some great relationships with the children. Now I’m there for about four hours a week and I always really look forward to going.

Because of what I’ve learnt at the children’s club, I’ve decided that when I finish school in three years’ time I want to go to college to become a qualified youth worker – someone who works with children. It wasn’t something I’d ever considered before starting at the club. In fact, I’d never thought about my future job at all. This has been my favourite part of the NYC so far and I’ve improved my own communication skills while at the same time helping others. I’ve got several other NYC certificates now and yes, one of them is for camping!


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: What was the main reason why Kyle joined the NYC?

options.A: to improve some of his skills
options.B: to reach the advanced level
options.C: to become a volunteer leader
options.D: to try new experiences

questions.explanation: 11. D: Trong đoạn 1, Kyle tham gia vì tổ chức này có các hoạt động như cắm trại, chèo thuyền... và đây là 'these were things I’d never done before' (những việc tôi chưa từng làm trước đây).

questions.text: Why didn’t Kyle want to start helping at the children’s club?

options.A: His memories of going there weren’t very positive.
options.B: He felt he was too shy to do a good job there.
options.C: He didn’t like the children there.
options.D: None of his friends worked there.

questions.explanation: 12. A: Ở đoạn 2, Kyle nhớ lại hồi 8 tuổi từng tham gia câu lạc bộ đó nhưng 'found it really difficult to make friends... so I’d stopped going' (rất khó kết bạn nên đã nghỉ đi), cho thấy những kỷ niệm không mấy vui vẻ.

questions.text: Kyle still goes to the children’s club because

options.A: he needs to earn his next certificate.
options.B: he enjoys spending time with the children.
options.C: his leader asked him to carry on.
options.D: his challenge is not yet finished.

questions.explanation: 13. B: Theo đoạn 3, sau khi hoàn thành thử thách, anh vẫn ở lại vì đã 'built up some great relationships with the children' (xây dựng được mối quan hệ tuyệt vời với bọn trẻ) và 'really look forward to going' (thực sự mong chờ được đến đó).

questions.text: Working at the children’s club has helped Kyle to

options.A: make a decision about his future career.
options.B: go on his first camping expedition.
options.C: get a place at college.
options.D: achieve his dream of working with children.

questions.explanation: 14. A: Đoạn cuối mô tả nhờ những gì học được ở câu lạc bộ, Kyle 'decided that when I finish school... I want to go to college to become a qualified youth worker' (đã quyết định sẽ học để trở thành một nhân viên công tác thanh thiếu niên).

questions.text: What would Kyle’s NYC leader say about him now?

options.A: Kyle’s learnt so much…
options.B: Even though Kyle’s reduced the number of hours…
options.C: Kyle’s a good example of why people shouldn’t be afraid…
options.D: Kyle’s become a different person…

questions.explanation: 15. C: Hành trình của Kyle chứng minh rằng dù ban đầu từng nhút nhát và sợ hãi ở câu lạc bộ này, nhưng khi dám đối mặt với thử thách mới, cậu không những vượt qua được mà còn cải thiện kỹ năng giao tiếp và tìm thấy đam mê.

questions.title: Cyclist Vicky Harmiston
questions.content_html:
Reporter Mark Lewis writes about Vicky Harmiston, who has had a successful career as a track cyclist – a cyclist who races on special race tracks.

When Vicky Harmiston was a child, her parents gave her and her brother Jamie the freedom to decide what they did in their spare time. Vicky chose to do lots of different sports. She was a good swimmer, and the coach at the swimming club she went to thought she might be good enough to become a champion. But the club was a long way from her home so it was difficult for her to fit in the training around her schoolwork. When they were teenagers, Jamie, who loved cycling, bought himself a special track-racing bike and started taking part in competitions. Vicky thought it looked very exciting and decided to try it for herself. She says that it was the best decision she ever made. Soon she was cycling every day and doing really well. The track was near her school, which meant it was no problem for her to attend training sessions after school every day.

Vicky went on to have a successful career in track cycling and won several competitions. Then, when she was 28, she retired from competitive cycling. Vicky told me: ‘For years I’d loved winning competitions but I began to get a bit tired of the whole thing – and when the excitement stops, there’s no point. Luckily, I went on to have a new career.’

Vicky got a job with a charity called CycleZone. ‘We work with young people who have never enjoyed sport,’ she says. ‘The first thing we do is teach them to ride a bike. We want them to learn to believe in themselves and their own abilities. CycleZone does a great job, and it gets young people together so they’re part of a wider group.’

The charity uses celebrities to advertise the work they do. Vicky says, ‘I know some people aren’t sure whether the support of a celebrity is always positive for a charity. They say that celebrities are only doing it to push themselves forward, which prevents the public from seeing the real work of the charity. But if famous singers and actors, for example, can help, I think they should.’


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: When Vicky first started cycling

options.A: she had a very good coach.
options.B: her parents gave her helpful advice.
options.C: she could get to a race track easily.
options.D: her brother gave her a great bike.

questions.explanation: 11. C: Trong đoạn 2 có câu 'The track was near her school, which meant it was no problem for her to attend training sessions after school every day' (Đường đua ở gần trường nên cô không gặp khó khăn gì khi tham gia các buổi tập mỗi ngày sau giờ học), cho thấy cô có thể đi đến đường đua rất dễ dàng.

questions.text: Why does Vicky say she stopped cycle racing?

options.A: She felt she was too old to do it.
options.B: She was becoming bored with it.
options.C: She had won everything she wanted.
options.D: She was preparing for a new career.

evidenceKeywords: get a bit tired of the whole thing
questions.explanation: 12. B: Ở đoạn 3, Vicky giải thích: 'I began to get a bit tired of the whole thing – and when the excitement stops, there’s no point' (Tôi bắt đầu thấy hơi chán mọi thứ – và khi sự hào hứng không còn nữa thì chẳng có ý nghĩa gì), chứng tỏ cô nghỉ vì bắt đầu thấy chán.

questions.text: What does the charity CycleZone do for young people?

options.A: It teaches them how to do track racing.
options.B: It supports those who have talent.
options.C: It offers them the chance to try a variety of sports.
options.D: It helps them become more confident.

questions.explanation: 13. D: Đoạn 4 nêu rõ mục tiêu của tổ chức từ thiện: 'We want them to learn to believe in themselves and their own abilities' (Chúng tôi muốn họ học cách tin tưởng vào bản thân và khả năng của chính mình), đồng nghĩa với việc giúp các bạn trẻ trở nên tự tin hơn.

questions.text: According to Vicky, some people believe that celebrities can

options.A: take attention away from what a charity does.
options.B: help people understand a charity’s work.
options.C: make the public care less about a charity.
options.D: encourage more people to become involved with a charity.

evidenceKeywords: prevents the public from seeing the real work
questions.explanation: 14. A: Trong đoạn cuối, Vicky kể lại ý kiến của một số người: 'They say that celebrities are only doing it to push themselves forward, which prevents the public from seeing the real work of the charity' (Họ cho rằng người nổi tiếng làm vậy chỉ để lăng xê bản thân, điều này ngăn cản công chúng nhìn thấy công việc thực sự của tổ chức từ thiện).

questions.text: What would Vicky say on her blog?

options.A: As a child, I always knew what I wanted to do when I grew up. But I never expected to become so famous.
options.B: If you join CycleZone, you’ll get to meet celebrities and learn how they’ve become successful.
options.C: Although track cycling is not the only sport I’ve been good at, I’ve never regretted my choice of career.
options.D: In my spare time I love going to schools and helping groups of children learn to ride bikes.

evidenceKeywords: chose to do lots of different sports
questions.explanation: 15. C: Bài đọc đề cập đến việc Vicky từng chơi nhiều môn thể thao và bơi rất giỏi (đoạn 1), sau đó cô quyết định theo đuổi đua xe đạp, mô tả đó là 'the best decision she ever made' (quyết định tuyệt vời nhất cô từng đưa ra) và có một sự nghiệp thành công.

questions.title: Basketball player
questions.content_html:
Luka Horvat writes about his early career.

My dad was a professional basketball player in Germany, as his father had been before him, and I went to watch many of his games when I was a kid. You might think that seeing so many matches would give me a love of the sport, but it actually had the opposite effect. I loved telling my friends how good my dad was, of course, especially when he won a game, but I used to take a book with me to read instead of watching.

Starting secondary school, I was still two years away from being a teenager but was already two metres tall. Seeing my height, my sports teacher asked if I’d be interested in training with the basketball team. Even though I enjoyed the session, I thought I’d need to develop my skills before I took part in a real match, but the teacher had more confidence in me than I did. It took me a while to agree, but a few weeks later I found myself playing against a team from another school. Mum and Dad coming to watch didn’t really help – it made me more nervous. But it was OK in the end!

For the next four years, I practised every day and did really well, even joining an adult team before I moved abroad to a special sports academy in the USA when I was fifteen. The coach there trains Olympic basketball players, and it was fantastic to work with him. However, I can’t say I enjoyed my first experience of living far from my parents. At home, I’d never been able to spend much time with my friends due to all the training, so that wasn’t such a change for me. I got used to everything about my new life in the end, though, and my English improved quickly too!

I turned professional at the age of eighteen, three years after arriving in the USA. I’d been taller than most players in the professional league since I was fifteen, but had been much too light for my height, so had to get that right first. My coach already knew a team that would take me while I was still at college, so I joined them and have never regretted it.


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: What does Luka say about his childhood?

options.A: He had a great interest in basketball.
options.B: He enjoyed watching his father play basketball.
options.C: He felt proud of his father’s success at basketball.
options.D: He knew he wanted to become a basketball player.

questions.explanation: 11. C: Trong đoạn 1, Luka nói: 'I loved telling my friends how good my dad was, of course, especially when he won a game' (Tất nhiên tôi rất thích kể cho bạn bè nghe bố tôi giỏi như thế nào, nhất là khi ông ấy thắng), thể hiện sự tự hào về thành công của bố.

questions.text: How did Luka feel before his first match at secondary school?

options.A: He wasn’t sure that he would do well.
options.B: He was pleased that his parents would be there.
options.C: He wasn’t happy about his teacher’s attitude.
options.D: He was delighted to be part of the team.

evidenceKeywords: thought I'd need to develop my skills
teacher had more confidence in me than I did
questions.explanation: 12. A: Ở đoạn 2, Luka chia sẻ: 'I thought I’d need to develop my skills before I took part in a real match, but the teacher had more confidence in me than I did' (Tôi nghĩ mình cần phát triển kỹ năng trước khi tham gia trận đấu thực sự, nhưng thầy giáo lại tự tin vào tôi hơn cả chính tôi), nghĩa là anh không chắc mình sẽ làm tốt.

questions.text: Luka thinks the most difficult thing about moving to the USA was

options.A: learning a new language.
options.B: being away from his family.
options.C: getting a good coach.
options.D: missing his friends.

questions.explanation: 13. B: Trong đoạn 3, Luka khẳng định: 'However, I can’t say I enjoyed my first experience of living far from my parents' (Tuy nhiên, tôi không thể nói là tôi thích trải nghiệm đầu tiên sống xa bố mẹ). Anh cũng nói thêm việc không ở cạnh bạn bè không phải là thay đổi lớn, tiếng Anh thì tiến bộ nhanh.

questions.text: What did Luka have to do before he became a professional player?

options.A: complete his studies
options.B: find a suitable team
options.C: reach a certain height
options.D: put on weight

evidenceKeywords: had to get that right first
questions.explanation: 14. D: Ở đoạn cuối, Luka kể lại: 'but had been much too light for my height, so had to get that right first' (nhưng tôi lại quá nhẹ cân so với chiều cao của mình, vì vậy phải giải quyết vấn đề đó trước tiên). 'Get that right' ở đây đồng nghĩa với việc phải tăng cân (put on weight).

questions.text: What would be a good way to introduce this article?

options.A: Luka Horvat has always been as interested in reading as in basketball…
options.B: Luka Horvat tells us how he became the latest member…
options.C: Professional basketball player Luka Horvat explains how luck…
options.D: Even though he only started playing basketball as a teenager…

questions.explanation: 15. B: Bài viết là tự truyện kể về hành trình từ tuổi thơ đến khi bắt đầu sự nghiệp của Luka Horvat, từ lúc tham gia đội bóng trường trung học cho đến khi chuyển sang Mỹ học viện và trở thành vận động viên chuyên nghiệp.

questions.title: Ana Ronson Singer-songwriter
questions.content_html:
Singer-songwriter Ana Ronson grew up in Ireland. Although her parents weren’t musicians, there was always music in the house. Her grandfather played the guitar, and taught Ana and her brother to play.

The first time Ana tried singing in front of an audience was at school – she was so nervous that her teacher had to lead her off the stage. This teacher suggested that joining the school theatre club might make her more confident. After attending the club for a while, she happily sang with 50 other students at an end-of-term concert.

A year later, her brother, who was in a band, asked her to write a song for them, and so she wrote her first ever song, Falling Stars. Writing it took just three days and she enjoyed it so much that she enrolled on a songwriting course run by a professional songwriter. Despite being the youngest student, she already knew more about music than many of the others. But Ana says the teacher didn’t listen to anything she said, and she wrote Something to Say about how annoyed this made her feel.

She posted this song online, and it became a huge success. She was delighted when people left comments saying that they loved it and found new meanings in the words each time they heard them. Joss Alton, the owner of a recording company called Isotope Music, flew from his office on the other side of Ireland to ask her to join Isotope. At first she wasn’t sure: she didn’t know anything about the company, and didn’t want someone telling her what to sing. However, Joss persuaded her this wouldn’t happen, and a short time later she performed at a concert in Dublin arranged by Isotope. They sold all the tickets very quickly and it was an amazing evening.

Ana says she’s less interested in writing songs about when life’s good; when it’s not, there’s more for her to say. One of her favourite songs is Decision, written about why her brother stopped singing for a while, and how unhappy it made him. While she hopes that fans will like her songs, her aim is to write about personal experiences that matter to her.


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: What helped Ana stop being scared of singing to an audience?

options.A: practising her singing with a band
options.B: asking a teacher to stand on stage with her
options.C: being in a drama group at school
options.D: making sure she was not in the front row

questions.explanation: 11. C: Trong đoạn 2 có nói 'This teacher suggested that joining the school theatre club might make her more confident' (Giáo viên gợi ý rằng việc tham gia câu lạc bộ kịch của trường có thể giúp cô tự tin hơn). Việc tham gia 'theatre club' tương đương với 'drama group'.

questions.text: How was the songwriting course useful for Ana?

options.A: She was able to meet some well-known singers.
options.B: The teacher’s attitude gave her an idea for a song.
options.C: She learnt a lot from other writers on the course.
options.D: The teacher gave her advice about the music business.

questions.explanation: 12. B: Ở đoạn 3, Ana nói rằng 'the teacher didn’t listen to anything she said, and she wrote Something to Say about how annoyed this made her feel' (giáo viên không nghe bất cứ điều gì cô nói, và cô đã viết bài Something to Say về việc điều này khiến cô bực mình ra sao). Sự bực mình từ thái độ của giáo viên đã gợi ý tưởng cho bài hát.

questions.text: Ana decided to work with Joss Alton because

options.A: he promised to let her choose which songs to sing.
options.B: he offered to help her put on a concert.
options.C: she liked some other singers that he worked with.
options.D: he owned a company in her home town.

evidenceKeywords: didn't want someone telling her what to sing
Joss persuaded her this wouldn't happen
questions.explanation: 13. A: Trong đoạn 4, Ana ban đầu không chắc chắn vì 'didn’t want someone telling her what to sing' (không muốn có người bảo cô phải hát gì). 'However, Joss persuaded her this wouldn’t happen' (Tuy nhiên, Joss đã thuyết phục cô rằng điều này sẽ không xảy ra), tức là ông hứa để cô tự chọn bài hát.

questions.text: Ana most enjoys writing songs that

options.A: she is sure her fans will like.
options.B: deal with difficult times.
options.C: her brother can sing with his band.
options.D: help people remember their own experiences.

evidenceKeywords: less interested in writing songs about when life’s good
when it's not, there's more for her to say
questions.explanation: 14. B: Đoạn cuối Ana chia sẻ: 'she’s less interested in writing songs about when life’s good; when it’s not, there’s more for her to say' (cô ít có hứng thú viết nhạc về những lúc cuộc sống tươi đẹp; khi cuộc sống không suôn sẻ, cô có nhiều điều để nói hơn), tức là cô thích viết về những khoảng thời gian khó khăn.

questions.text: What would Ana write to fans in her blog?

options.A: Listening to my parents playing music…
options.B: Writing songs just gets easier with practice…
options.C: I loved playing the concert in Dublin…
options.D: I read what people write about me online…

evidenceKeywords: found new meanings in the words
questions.explanation: 15. D: Trong đoạn 4, bài hát online của cô trở nên cực kỳ thành công và 'She was delighted when people left comments saying that they loved it' (Cô rất vui mừng khi mọi người để lại bình luận nói rằng họ yêu thích bài hát), chứng tỏ cô có đọc những gì người khác viết về mình trên mạng.

questions.title: My Canadian trip
questions.content_html:
Last year I went on an amazing trip – travelling by boat on a guided group tour along the west coast of Canada. It was my brother Harry’s idea. He’s a journalist, like me, and he wanted to write articles about the trip. He’s also a great fan of boats, although that’s one interest we definitely don’t share. But I’d dreamt of visiting the area ever since seeing it on TV as a child, especially as I knew it was where our great-grandparents had lived before moving to Europe. So I kept asking Harry if I could go too – until he finally agreed!

A few weeks before we left home, there were storms in the area we were going to. But luckily the forecast for the time we intended to be there was for calm seas. Although there was plenty to arrange, I was busy at work so didn’t have much time to think about what needed doing. But Harry promised he’d taken care of everything, so I knew everything would be all right.

After arriving in Canada, we joined the group, packed our limited supplies into small boats and set off. The guide had mentioned that very few people now lived along that coast, and sure enough, the only other living creatures we saw for the first few days were dolphins and birds. We knew there were islands in the distance, but the early-morning fog made it hard to see very far, so I just focused on the beautiful patterns our boat made in the water.

We often stopped for hours to explore the rock pools on the beaches. They were full of amazing coloured fish, many of which I didn’t recognise. And it was great to be able to stop caring about how quickly or slowly the day was passing. We forgot lunch or dinner, though, which we all made together over camp fires. When we finally fell asleep on the boats each evening, even though the beds were hard, it really felt like stress-free living!

When the time came to leave, I was sad. How could I return to normal life again? But I knew if I stayed, I’d miss family and friends. I was also looking forward to telling everyone at home about our adventures!


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: Why was Louise keen to go on the trip to Canada?

options.A: She liked the idea of spending time in a boat.
options.B: She knew her brother wanted her to accompany him.
options.C: She had wanted to travel there for a long time.
options.D: She had heard from some relatives who lived there.

evidenceKeywords: ever since seeing it on TV as a child
questions.explanation: 11. C: Trong đoạn 1, Louise tiết lộ: 'I’d dreamt of visiting the area ever since seeing it on TV as a child' (Tôi đã mơ ước được đến thăm khu vực này kể từ khi nhìn thấy nó trên TV lúc còn nhỏ), chứng tỏ cô đã muốn du lịch đến đây từ rất lâu.

questions.text: Just before their departure, Louise

options.A: began to wonder how they would deal with bad weather.
options.B: was confident that they were fully prepared for the trip.
options.C: wished she could help her brother more.
options.D: felt she was better organised than usual.

evidenceKeywords: Harry promised he’d taken care of everything
knew everything would be all right
questions.explanation: 12. B: Ở đoạn 2, vì bận rộn công việc nên cô không có thời gian chuẩn bị, nhưng 'Harry promised he’d taken care of everything, so I knew everything would be all right' (Harry hứa anh ấy sẽ lo liệu mọi thứ, vì vậy tôi biết mọi việc sẽ ổn thỏa), thể hiện sự tự tin rằng chuyến đi đã được chuẩn bị đầy đủ.

questions.text: On the first morning of the trip, Louise says she admired

options.A: the way the sea around them looked.
options.B: the wildlife which their boats attracted.
options.C: the homes that people had built in the area.
options.D: the views of islands they were passing.

evidenceKeywords: fog made it hard to see
questions.explanation: 13. A: Trong đoạn 3, vì sương mù buổi sớm khiến họ không thể nhìn xa, nên Louise chia sẻ: 'so I just focused on the beautiful patterns our boat made in the water' (tôi chỉ tập trung vào những hoa văn tuyệt đẹp mà con thuyền tạo ra trên mặt nước).

questions.text: During the trip, Louise enjoyed

options.A: learning the names of the fish she saw.
options.B: not having to cook regular meals.
options.C: spending the nights in comfort.
options.D: not having to worry about time.

evidenceKeywords: how quickly or slowly the day was passing
questions.explanation: 14. D: Tại đoạn 4, Louise miêu tả: 'And it was great to be able to stop caring about how quickly or slowly the day was passing' (Và thật tuyệt khi có thể không phải quan tâm xem ngày trôi qua nhanh hay chậm như thế nào).

questions.text: What would Louise write in her diary during the trip?

options.A: There are wonderful pools along the coast…
options.B: I can’t believe I’m in the same place I saw…
options.C: We’ve brought a lot of stuff with us…
options.D: It’ll be hard to say goodbye…

evidenceKeywords: ever since seeing it on TV as a child
questions.explanation: 15. B: Vì từ nhỏ Louise đã thấy vùng đất này trên TV và mơ ước được tới đó (đoạn 1), việc viết nhật ký thể hiện sự bất ngờ và hạnh phúc khi được thực sự đặt chân đến nơi mình từng thấy hoàn toàn phù hợp với cảm xúc của chuyến đi.
